name: Successful Lateral Movement to Administrator via Pass the Hash using Mimikatz Detected
description: Detects a successful 'Pass the Hash' lateral movement attack. This rule identifies a sequence of events where a user logs on with a `Logon Type 9` (NewCredentials), a strong indicator of this attack, followed by the assignment of new privileges, which suggests a compromise of an administrator account.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: A successful lateral movement attempt to an administrator account was detected using a 'Pass the Hash' technique. A logon event with `Logon Type 9` was observed for user **{user}** ({user_id}) from domain **{domain}**, followed by a privilege assignment. This is a strong indicator of a **Lateral Movement** attack (**MITRE ATT&CK T1550.002**), where an adversary has likely compromised an administrator account. Immediate investigation is required to determine the full scope of the compromise and to reset the credentials of the affected account.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The name of the user account that was compromised.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The user ID of the compromised account.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: The domain of the compromised account.
